message: Your pace was slow at {wpm} WPM. Try speaking with more energy and urgency.
tip: Practice timed sprints: set a 30-second timer and speak as fast as you can while staying clear.

message: You spoke very fast at {wpm} WPM. Slow down to let your audience absorb your message.
tip: Try the 'pause power' technique: stop for 1 second after every key point.

message: Excellent pace at {wpm} WPM! You are in the sweet spot for audience engagement.
tip: Keep practicing at this pace to make it your natural rhythm.

message: Good pace at {wpm} WPM. You are close to the ideal range of 120-160.
tip: Try adjusting slightly toward 130-140 WPM for maximum impact.

message: You used {fillerCount} filler words: {fillerWords}. This undermines your credibility.
tip: Replace fillers with intentional pauses. Silence shows confidence.

message: You used {fillerCount} filler words: {fillerWords}. Aim for zero in your next session.
tip: Record yourself for 30 seconds daily and consciously eliminate one filler at a time.

message: Zero filler words! Your speech was clean and confident.
tip: You are building excellent habits. Maintain this in longer sessions.

message: Outstanding vocabulary! You used {advancedWordCount} advanced words: {advancedWords}.
tip: You are a word master. Keep challenging yourself with complex prompts.

message: Good vocabulary usage with {advancedWordCount} advanced words: {advancedWords}.
tip: Try to use 1-2 more advanced words in your next session.

message: Your vocabulary was basic. Challenge yourself with more sophisticated words.
tip: Pick 3 words from our advanced list and intentionally use them next time.

message: Your volume was low. Speak louder to project confidence and authority.
tip: Stand up straight, take a deep breath, and imagine speaking to someone 20 feet away.

message: Good vocal presence. Your volume shows moderate confidence.
tip: Project just a bit more to command the room completely.

message: Strong vocal presence! Your volume and energy command attention.
tip: You have great vocal power. Now focus on varying your tone to keep it engaging.

message: Your tone was flat. Varying your pitch makes speech more engaging.
tip: Try the 'siren' exercise: slide your voice from low to high pitch for 30 seconds daily.

message: Decent tonal variety. Your voice had some natural variation.
tip: Emphasize key words by slightly raising your pitch on them.

message: Excellent vocal variety! Your dynamic tone keeps listeners engaged.
tip: You have great tonal range. Maintain this while controlling your pace.

message: You had {pauseCount} long pauses. Frequent pauses can signal uncertainty.
tip: Prepare a mental outline before speaking so you always know what comes next.

message: You used {pauseCount} strategic pauses. Well-placed pauses add emphasis.
tip: Your pauses are good. Make sure they are intentional, not hesitant.

message: No awkward pauses! Your fluency was impressive.
tip: You have excellent flow. Try using intentional pauses for dramatic effect.
